pure_fr.evidence: Output A presents a clear, atomic FR focused on measurement and reporting of energy per unit work output, maintaining solution neutrality and testability with explicit verification ideas. Output B conflates multiple concerns (monitoring, control, safety integration) in a non-atomic FR, requiring further decomposition and embedding solution and control aspects prematurely.

fr_dp_bridge.evidence: Output A demonstrates strong adherence to the Independence Axiom by decomposing energy consumption and work output into two independent measurement streams unified only at the reporting layer, minimizing coupling and complexity. It clearly separates what (FR) from how (DP) with minimal diffusion risk and well-defined interfaces. Output B addresses a broader, less atomic FR with a hybrid DP that integrates monitoring, control, and feedback, resulting in higher coupling potential and complexity; while interfaces are defined, the DP mixes solution concepts and outcome enforcement, reducing clarity on what-vs-how boundaries.

dp_im_bridge.evidence: Output A demonstrates excellent containment of DP5 within a single NGHE Solution module with minimal diffusion (containment score 0.92, diffusion 0.08), supported by clear interface boundaries and detailed risk mitigations. Output B also shows strong containment (95/100) with well-defined interfaces and centralized analytics, but some diffusion risk remains due to multi-module coordination and advisory feedback delivery, slightly lowering containment purity.

pure_im.evidence: Output A provides detailed structural modularity evidence with quantified containment (0.92) and diffusion (0.08) scores, clear architectural boundaries, and explicit risk mitigation strategies, demonstrating strong cohesion and stability of the NGHE Solution module. Output B also shows strong containment (95/100) and well-defined interfaces but is less detailed on internal modularity optimization and clustering metrics, with some risks related to potential bottlenecks and complexity in real-time feedback integration.

fr_im_bridge.evidence: Output A demonstrates clear, well-defined IM ownership with NGHE Solution encapsulating measurement orchestration and reporting, explicit interface contracts, and verifiable acceptance criteria enabling traceable verification at the IM boundary. Output B shows a broader system-level IM ownership with multiple interfaces and coordination but lacks atomicity in FR, has higher complexity in feedback loops, and some risks in latency and safety integration, reducing clarity and verifiability at the FR-IM boundary.
